matches.match_rationale: Education operators lack structured career pathway documentation — competency frameworks, leadership readiness rubrics, mentorship guides, and individualized development plans. Text generation can produce all of these artifacts from a few inputs about the teacher's current role, years of experience, and target leadership track. This directly addresses the absence of formal scaffolding rather than just filling a cultural gap.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool that generates personalized leadership development plans (IDP) for teachers, including milestone competencies, recommended readings, and coaching prompts — configured per campus and aligned to the operator's role hierarchy.

matches.match_rationale: Leadership readiness is assessed informally by principals today, introducing bias and inconsistency. A structured classification model trained on observable teacher attributes — observation scores, years experience, committee involvement, peer feedback counts, PD hours — can score each teacher against a 'readiness for leadership role' label. This mechanizes the readiness signal that currently doesn't exist.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A leadership readiness classifier that ingests existing HR and observation data to rank teachers by their probability of succeeding in dept head or curriculum specialist roles, surfacing candidates principals may have overlooked.

matches.match_rationale: High turnover of first-time leaders is the stated acute pain. Propensity modeling can predict which newly appointed leaders are at elevated attrition risk within their first 12 months based on preparation factors, support levels, workload signals, and engagement data. Early identification enables targeted interventions — coaching, reduced load, mentorship matching — before the departure happens.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An early-warning model that flags new leaders at risk of departure within 6 months, integrating HR data on role transition, performance reviews, and engagement pulse scores to trigger a coaching or check-in workflow.

matches.match_rationale: New leaders in education have no internal knowledge repository to draw on — they must learn by trial and error or rely on overburdened principals. A RAG system over curated leadership development resources, internal policy documents, and peer case studies can serve as an on-demand leadership coach. This addresses both the absence of mentorship culture and the unpreparedness of new leaders without requiring human availability at scale.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An internal leadership knowledge assistant that new dept heads can query for guidance on difficult scenarios (e.g., managing underperforming staff, running curriculum reviews) — grounded in the operator's own policies and curated leadership literature.

matches.match_rationale: Coaching and mentorship culture cannot scale with human mentors alone across 500+ staff and multiple campuses. A conversational coaching agent can simulate structured mentoring conversations — surfacing reflection prompts, goal-tracking questions, and development feedback — accessible to every aspiring leader asynchronously. This directly substitutes for the absent mentorship infrastructure.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A leadership coaching chatbot for aspiring and newly promoted leaders that guides weekly check-ins, tracks progress against their development plan, and prompts reflection using evidence-based coaching frameworks (e.g., GROW model).

matches.match_rationale: Building a leadership pipeline requires matching aspiring leaders to mentors, development opportunities, and role rotations — all subject to hard constraints like campus availability, subject expertise, workload limits, and seniority rules. Constraint satisfaction can generate valid mentor-mentee pairings and rotation assignments that principals currently do ad hoc or not at all.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A pipeline matching tool that assigns aspiring leaders to mentors and shadow opportunities across campuses, satisfying constraints on expertise alignment, schedule, and campus distribution — producing a structured cohort plan each term.
